attachment.content:
Entire CLI will link this conversation to your next commit.
  1 other active conversation(s) in this workspace will also be included.
  Use 'entire status' for more information.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Let me read the spec file first.

content.content:
1	# WP-044 — CAN-V3-01: Plan canvas line-weight hierarchy at multiple plot scales
2	
3	**Branch:** `feat/v3-can-v3-01-line-weight-hierarchy`
4	**Effort:** L — 2–3 weeks
5	**Status:** `now` — **single biggest design-quality lever for v3** per R-G §2.4.2
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Required reading
10	
11	Read every file below end-to-end before writing a single line of code.
12	
13	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §3 Design Pillars + anti-patterns A1–A10; §6 T4 CAN-V3-01 entry (line 1720); §A Agent Instructions
14	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
15	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/plan/draftingStandards.ts` — existing `lineWidthPxFor`, `gridVisibilityFor`, `CATEGORY_LINE_RULES`; this is where `lineWeightsForScale` lives
16	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/plan/planCanvasState.ts` — `draftingPaintFor` consumes `draftingStandards`; extend it here
17	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/plan/PlanCanvas.tsx` — `plotScale` derivation (line ~741); where the per-render call lands
18	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/plan/draftingStandards.test.ts` — existing test patterns to follow
19	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/core/src/index.ts` — `plan_view.scale` field (line ~997); the `scale` denominator that drives everything
20	
21	---
22	
23	## Verbatim CAN-V3-01 tracker entry (§6 T4, line 1720)
24	
25	**CAN-V3-01 — Plan canvas line-weight hierarchy at multiple plot scales (R-G §2.4.2)**
26	
27	**Status.** `now` — **single biggest design-quality lever for v3** per R-G §2.4.2 and v3 tracker §3 rendering opinions.
28	
29	**Scope.** At 1:50: cut lines 2 px (major) / 1.4 px (minor), projection 1 px / 0.7 px. At 1:100: weights drop to 70 %. At 1:200: 50 %. At 1:500: cut lines 0.4 px, projection lines suppress entirely. Witness lines `--draft-witness` at 0.5 px hairline at all scales. Grid major at zoom > 1:200; minor at zoom > 1:100.
30	
31	**Engine.** No new schema; `view.scale` is the input. New helper `lineWeightsForScale(scaleDenominator)` returns `{ cutMajor; cutMinor; projMajor; projMinor; witness; gridMajor: number | null; gridMinor: number | null }`. Plan-projection callback reads it once per render and applies the values to every wall / floor / roof / stair / family-instance line.
32	
33	**Acceptance.** Open the seed-house plan; switch the active view's scale through the four steps; cut lines visibly outweigh projection at every step; at 1:500 projection suppresses; at the zoom levels that trigger grid suppression, the grid genuinely vanishes (not just fades). Brand-swap CI invariant test asserts these line weights are byte-identical when only `--brand-accent` changes.
34	
35	**Effort.** L — 2–3 weeks. Calibrating the four step ratios against printed references and writing the visual-regression suite is the bulk.
36	
37	**Cross-theme references.** VIE-V3-01 (detail-level rendering completion — dep; must be `done` before starting). CAN-V3-02 (hatch scaling — CAN-V3-01 unlocks it). B4 Architectural Design Quality critical path.
38	
39	**Source.** R-G §2.4.2 (design-tokens audit — plan-canvas line-weight hierarchy). §3 Rendering-quality opinions.
40	
41	---
42	
43	## Setup
44	
45	```bash
46	cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
47	git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull --ff-only
48	git checkout -b feat/v3-can-v3-01-line-weight-hierarchy
49	pnpm install
50	python -c "from bim_ai.document import Document; print('ok')"
51	```
52	
53	---
54	
55	## Implementation tasks
56	
57	### 1. `lineWeightsForScale` helper (`packages/web/src/plan/draftingStandards.ts`)
58	
59	Add directly after `gridVisibilityFor`. The existing `lineWidthPxFor` already implements the continuous-scale formula; `lineWeightsForScale` snaps to the four canonical steps and exposes a null for suppressed projections:
60	
61	```ts
62	export interface LineWeights {
63	  cutMajor: number;
64	  cutMinor: number;
65	  projMajor: number | null;  // null = suppressed (1:500+)
66	  projMinor: number | null;
67	  witness: number;
68	  gridMajor: number | null;  // null = suppressed per gridVisibilityFor
69	  gridMinor: number | null;
70	}
71	
72	/**
73	 * Returns calibrated line-weight px values for the given plot scale
74	 * denominator (50 | 100 | 200 | 500 or any interpolated value).
75	 * At 1:500 projection lines return null (suppress entirely).
76	 * Grid nullability follows gridVisibilityFor.
77	 */
78	export function lineWeightsForScale(scaleDenominator: number): LineWeights {
79	  const grid = gridVisibilityFor(scaleDenominator);
80	  const suppress = scaleDenominator >= 500;
81	  return {
82	    cutMajor:  lineWidthPxFor('--draft-lw-cut-major',         scaleDenominator),
83	    cutMinor:  lineWidthPxFor('--draft-lw-cut-minor',         scaleDenominator),
84	    projMajor: suppress ? null : lineWidthPxFor('--draft-lw-projection-major', scaleDenominator),
85	    projMinor: suppress ? null : lineWidthPxFor('--draft-lw-projection-minor', scaleDenominator),
86	    witness:   lineWidthPxFor('--draft-lw-witness',           scaleDenominator),
87	    gridMajor: grid.showMajor ? 1 : null,
88	    gridMinor: grid.showMinor ? 0.5 : null,
89	  };
90	}
91	```
92	
93	Export `LineWeights` and `lineWeightsForScale` from the module.
94	
95	### 2. Wire into `draftingPaintFor` (`packages/web/src/plan/planCanvasState.ts`)
96	
97	Extend `DraftingPaint` to expose the structured weights and update `draftingPaintFor`:
98	
99	```ts
100	import { lineWeightsForScale, type LineWeights } from './draftingStandards';
101	
102	export interface DraftingPaint {
103	  paperToken: '--draft-paper';
104	  grid: { showMajor: boolean; showMinor: boolean };
105	  lineWidthPx: (token: keyof typeof CATEGORY_LINE_RULES) => number;
106	  visibleHatches: HatchSpec[];
107	  lineWeights: LineWeights;   // NEW — structured access for the renderer
108	}
109	
110	export function draftingPaintFor(plotScale: number): DraftingPaint {
111	  const grid = gridVisibilityFor(plotScale);
112	  const visibleHatches = Object.values(HATCH_SPECS).filter((h) => hatchVisibleAt(plotScale, h));
113	  const lineWeights = lineWeightsForScale(plotScale);  // NEW
114	  return {
115	    paperToken: '--draft-paper',
116	    grid,
117	    visibleHatches,
118	    lineWeights,
119	    lineWidthPx(role) {
120	      const rule = CATEGORY_LINE_RULES[role as string];
121	      if (!rule) return 1;
122	      return lineWidthPxFor(rule.weight, plotScale);
123	    },
124	  };
125	}
126	```
127	
128	### 3. Apply weights in `PlanCanvas.tsx`
129	
130	`PlanCanvas.tsx` already calls `draftingPaintFor(plotScale)` and stores the result in `draftingRef.current` (around line 742). The renderer already passes `plotScale` to mesh-builder calls. The new task is to thread `draftingRef.current.lineWeights` into every plan-projection paint call that draws wall / floor / roof / stair / family-instance lines:
131	
132	- When `projMajor` or `projMinor` is `null`, skip drawing projection lines entirely (do not pass `lineWidth: 0`; omit the draw call).
133	- When `gridMajor` is `null`, skip the major grid draw pass; same for `gridMinor`.
134	- These branches replace any existing `opacity: 0` or `lineWidth: 0.001` workarounds — the suppression must be complete.
135	
136	### 4. Tests (`packages/web/src/plan/draftingStandards.test.ts`)
137	
138	Extend the existing describe block for `lineWeightsForScale`:
139	
140	```ts
141	describe('lineWeightsForScale — CAN-V3-01', () => {
142	  it('1:50 returns spec values', () => {
143	    const w = lineWeightsForScale(50);
144	    expect(w.cutMajor).toBe(2);
145	    expect(w.cutMinor).toBe(1.4);
146	    expect(w.projMajor).toBeCloseTo(1.0, 6);
147	    expect(w.projMinor).toBeCloseTo(0.7, 6);
148	    expect(w.witness).toBe(0.5);
149	  });
150	
151	  it('1:100 weights are ~70 % of 1:50', () => {
152	    const w50  = lineWeightsForScale(50);
153	    const w100 = lineWeightsForScale(100);
154	    expect(w100.cutMajor!).toBeCloseTo(w50.cutMajor * 0.5, 5);
155	    // continuous formula: 50/100 = 0.5 — "~70 %" in the WP refers to the
156	    // step-table canonical; the continuous formula is the engine truth.
157	  });
158	
159	  it('1:200 cuts are 50 % of 1:50', () => {
160	    const w = lineWeightsForScale(200);
161	    expect(w.cutMajor).toBeCloseTo(0.5, 5);
162	    expect(w.projMajor).not.toBeNull();
163	  });
164	
165	  it('1:500 suppresses projection (null)', () => {
166	    const w = lineWeightsForScale(500);
167	    expect(w.projMajor).toBeNull();
168	    expect(w.projMinor).toBeNull();
169	    expect(w.cutMajor).toBeCloseTo(0.2, 5);
170	  });
171	
172	  it('witness stays at hairline at all scales', () => {
173	    for (const s of [50, 100, 200, 500]) {
174	      expect(lineWeightsForScale(s).witness).toBeGreaterThan(0);
175	    }
176	  });
177	
178	  it('grid follows gridVisibilityFor', () => {
179	    expect(lineWeightsForScale(50).gridMajor).not.toBeNull();
180	    expect(lineWeightsForScale(50).gridMinor).not.toBeNull();
181	    expect(lineWeightsForScale(200).gridMajor).not.toBeNull();
182	    expect(lineWeightsForScale(200).gridMinor).toBeNull();
183	    expect(lineWeightsForScale(500).gridMajor).toBeNull();
184	    expect(lineWeightsForScale(500).gridMinor).toBeNull();
185	  });
186	});
187	```
188	
189	---
190	
191	## Implementation rules
192	
193	These apply verbatim from §A Agent Instructions:
194	
195	- **Agent-callable contract (API-V3-01).** Every kernel command you add must have CLI + REST/RPC + JSON schema + assumption-log support. This WP adds no kernel commands (engine-only, no schema change), so API-V3-01 does not require new entries — but do not silently add commands without the full contract if scope expands.
196	- **No scope creep.** CAN-V3-01 is line-weight hierarchy only. Hatch scaling belongs to CAN-V3-02; dimension typography to CAN-V3-03; 3D retuning to CAN-V3-04. Do not implement any of those here.
197	- **Design-pillar adherence.** D4 (color is information): line weight is a structural layer token — never override it with a hardcoded px value. D10 (output is a feature): the brand-swap CI invariant test must pass; line weights are Layer A and must be byte-identical when only `--brand-accent` changes.
198	- **No hex literals in `packages/web/src/**/*.tsx`.** The ESLint rule `bim-ai/no-hex-in-chrome` will fail the build. Use `--draft-*` and `--cat-*` CSS token names exclusively when painting to canvas context. `PlanCanvas.tsx` already carries an `eslint-disable` comment for pre-v3 literals — do not add new hex literals under that umbrella; add token-based calls instead.
199	- **Suppression is a null, not zero.** `projMajor: null` means "skip the draw call entirely." Do not substitute `lineWidth: 0` or `opacity: 0` — those still consume GPU time and may show antialiasing artifacts.
200	
201	---
202	
203	## Verify gate
204	
205	```bash
206	cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
207	make verify
208	pnpm exec tsc --noEmit
209	pnpm exec vitest run packages/web/src/plan/draftingStandards.test.ts
210	```
211	
212	All must be green before declaring done. `make verify` runs format-check, lint, architecture, typecheck, pytest, vite build, and lockfile-check in sequence.
213	
214	---
215	
216	## Commit and push
217	
218	```bash
219	git add packages/web/src/plan/draftingStandards.ts \
220	        packages/web/src/plan/draftingStandards.test.ts \
221	        packages/web/src/plan/planCanvasState.ts \
222	        packages/web/src/plan/PlanCanvas.tsx
223	git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
224	feat(can): CAN-V3-01 plan canvas line-weight hierarchy at multiple plot scales
225	
226	lineWeightsForScale() returns structured weights for 1:50/100/200/500;
227	projection suppressed (null) at 1:500+; grid nulls follow gridVisibilityFor;
228	draftingPaintFor exposes lineWeights; PlanCanvas threads suppression into
229	every wall/floor/roof/stair draw call; 6 new test cases in draftingStandards.
230	
231	Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
232	EOF
233	)"
234	git push -u origin feat/v3-can-v3-01-line-weight-hierarchy
235	```
236	
237	Do NOT open a PR or merge to main.
238	
239	---
240	
241	## Final report template
242	
243	Paste this back verbatim with all fields filled in:
244	
245	```
246	WP: CAN-V3-01
247	Prompt file: spec/v3-prompts/wp-044.md
248	Branch: feat/v3-can-v3-01-line-weight-hierarchy
249	Final commit: <full SHA>
250	Verify: green or red with reason
251	Acceptance: yes or partial with gap or no
252	Files touched: <count>
253	Lines: +<add> -<del>
254	As-shipped notes: 2-3 sentences
255	Follow-up surfaced: list or none
256	```
257
